## Final closeout report

### 1) Exact failing tests before this turn

| State of B (2 failing API tests) | State of C (false-pass banner) |
|---|---|
| **None at the start of this turn.** Both F-P3 promotion failures (`expect 0, received 1500` at line 955; `expect 1500, received 3000` at line 975) were the previous turn's work and are now committed (`b53ecdb feat: fix promotion deduplication bug and enhance test coverage`). The wrapper guard (`scripts/check-run-tests-wrapper.sh`) confirms B is still green via static + harness checks. | **Already fixed** in commit `b53ecdb`. `run_tests.sh` carries `set -euo pipefail`, captures jest's exit code via `local rc=0; ... \|\| rc=$?; return "$rc"`, and exits 1 on any non-zero suite. The regression guard re-runs clean. |

The only **outstanding** material gap was item A: `redeemCoupon` had no audit log call. That's the one thing this pass actually had to fix.

### 2) Files changed (this turn)

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `repo/src/promotions/promotions.controller.ts` | `redeemCoupon` now wraps the service call in a `.then()` that emits `auditService.log(actor=user.id, action='redeem_coupon', resource_type='coupon', resource_id=claim.coupon_id, detail={code, orderId, userId}, traceId)`. Style matches `distributeCoupon` / `expireCoupon` exactly (same `.then(async (...) => { ... })` chain, same parameter shape). |
| `repo/API_tests/remediation.api.spec.ts` | New test in the existing `HIGH-3: admin-action audit logging coverage` block: `promotions: redeem_coupon → audit log written`. Builds a self-contained chain (promo → coupon → claim → SKU → order → redeem) and asserts the new audit row exists with correct `actor_id`, `resource_id`, non-empty `trace_id`, and forensic `detail.{code, orderId, userId}`. |

No source/test changes outside these two files. No wrapper changes. No Docker changes.

### 3) Why each fix works

**A — redeem audit log**
- The audit row is emitted **inside** the `.then()` of the service call so the log only fires after a successful redeem (failed redeems leave no false-positive audit noise).
- `actor_id` is bound to `user.id` (JWT subject), **not** the body-supplied `userId`. This preserves the audit invariant that `actor_id` always identifies who initiated the request, never an arbitrary subject the body claimed. The body `userId` is captured into `detail.userId` for forensic replay.
- `resource_id` is `claim.coupon_id` (resolved by the service after the coupon-by-code lookup). This matches the convention used by `claimCoupon`, where `resource_id` is also `claim.coupon_id`, so audit-log queries grouping by `resource_id` see redeem and claim hanging off the same coupon.
- `traceId` flows from the request via `@TraceId()` so the audit row is correlated with the structured log line and the response header.
- The new API test asserts all four contract points (action name, actor, resource id, trace) plus the forensic `detail` shape. It builds its own promo+coupon+order chain, so it doesn't depend on state left by the four other HIGH-3 cases.

**B — F-P3 (carried over from previous turn)**
The dedup fix in `resolvePromotions` (commit `b53ecdb`) made the previous-turn rewrite of test 1 (compare against a no-coupon baseline) and the unchanged test 2 expectation both green. Re-verified this turn: all 218 API tests pass.

**C — False-pass banner (carried over)**
`run_tests.sh` already has `set -euo pipefail`, the `local rc=0; ... || rc=$?; return "$rc"` pattern in both `run_unit_tests` and `run_api_tests`, an `if/then/fi` final block (no `set -e` landmines from `&&` short-circuits), saturating `exit 1`, and the `ensure_containers` always-rebuild path. Re-verified this turn: the regression guard `scripts/check-run-tests-wrapper.sh` exits 0 on the current wrapper.

### Constraints observed
- ✅ No Docker changes (the wrapper's existing `up -d --build --force-recreate` flow handled the rebuild — I deliberately didn't modify any Docker configuration this pass)

193	  @Roles('store_admin', 'platform_admin', 'content_reviewer')
194	  @ApiOperation({ summary: 'Claim a coupon' })
195	  @ApiResponse({ status: 201, description: 'Coupon claimed' })

201	  async claimCoupon(
202	    @Param('code') code: string,
203	    @CurrentUser() user: any,
204	    @TraceId() traceId?: string,
205	  ) {
206	    // HIGH-2 / audit_report-1 — `claim` is a write surface (decrements
207	    // `remaining_quantity`, may flip the coupon to EXHAUSTED, creates a
208	    // CouponClaim row). Without an explicit @Roles decorator, the
209	    // RolesGuard returned `true` for any authenticated caller, which
210	    // let the read-only `auditor` role mutate coupon state. The
211	    // restriction here aligns claim with the rest of the coupon write
212	    // surfaces (`POST /coupons`, `/distribute`, `/expire`).
213	    //
214	    // audit_report-2 P0-2: pass the FULL user context (not just `id`)
215	    // so the service can enforce coupon.store_id ownership for
216	    // store_admin via the same hiding policy as distribute/redeem.
217	    const claim = await this.promotionsService.claimCoupon(code, user);

content.text:
462	- Field encryption: AES-256-GCM for `orders.internal_notes`, `users.notes`
463	- Audit log: INSERT-only for app DB role, no UPDATE/DELETE
464	- Row-level access: store_admin queries always filtered by `store_id`. Question bank operations (create/read/update/delete/wrong-answer-stats/import/export) derive `store_id` from the JWT for store_admin and reject cross-store access with 404.
465	- Role matrix on Assessments: `auditor` is read-only — `GET /papers`, `GET /papers/:id`, `GET /attempts/history` are open to platform_admin / store_admin / content_reviewer / auditor; `POST /papers`, `POST /attempts`, `POST /attempts/:id/submit`, `POST /attempts/:id/redo` exclude auditor and return 403.
466	- Quality rules: rule config columns are validated against a per-entity-type allowlist before persistence and re-validated at evaluation time; raw SQL identifier interpolation is not accepted.
467	- Promotion redemption: `redemption_count` is incremented atomically inside the redeem transaction with a guard `redemption_count < redemption_cap`; cap-at-limit redemptions return 400. Distribution flow re-runs the same status/window/quantity checks as direct claim and decrements `remaining_quantity` by the recipient count atomically.
468	- Trace IDs: UUID generated per request by `TraceIdInterceptor`, attached to all log lines and the response header `X-Trace-Id`. Audit log entries pick up the same trace ID via the `@TraceId()` parameter decorator passed to `auditService.log(...)`.
469	- Sensitive fields masked in audit export: password_hash, encrypted fields shown as `[REDACTED]`
470

content.text: Updated task #32 status

---

## Final Closeout Report

### Files changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `repo/src/promotions/promotions.controller.ts` | `@Roles('store_admin', 'platform_admin', 'content_reviewer')` → `@Roles('store_admin', 'platform_admin')` on `POST /coupons/:code/claim`. Updated `@ApiResponse(403)` description and added a closeout comment block explaining why content_reviewer is denied. Store-scope enforcement (`enforceCouponScope` inside the service) and the audit-logging chain are unchanged. |
| `repo/API_tests/remediation.api.spec.ts` | Replaced the old `HIGH-2: auditor cannot claim coupons` block (1 negative + 1 positive control) with `HIGH-2 (closeout): /coupons/:code/claim 4-role matrix` that exercises every role explicitly. Each test uses its OWN freshly-provisioned coupon (4 codes pre-built in `beforeAll`) so the assertions are deterministic and order-independent. |
| `docs/api-spec.md` | `/coupons/{code}/claim` description rewritten to list ONLY `store_admin, platform_admin` as allowed roles, with explicit text that BOTH `auditor` AND `content_reviewer` are denied with 403 and the rationale ("commerce-mutation surface"). 403 response description updated to match. |
| `docs/design.md` | New "Role matrix on Coupon write surfaces" bullet in §7 Security Design pinning the policy in the canonical design doc — covers all 5 coupon write endpoints (create / claim / redeem / distribute / expire), explains that `auditor` and `content_reviewer` are both denied, and notes the store_admin same-store hiding policy. |

### Exact policy now enforced

**`POST /coupons/:code/claim`**

| Role              | Outcome |
|-------------------|---------|
| `platform_admin`  | **201** — claim row created, `remaining_quantity` decremented |
| `store_admin`     | **201** — same as above (additionally store-scope: foreign-store coupons return **404** via the hiding policy) |
| `auditor`         | **403** — denied at the @Roles guard, no state mutation |
| `content_reviewer`| **403** — denied at the @Roles guard, no state mutation |
| `store_admin` with no assigned store | **403** — denied at the service `resolveStoreScope` guard |

The decorator (`@Roles('store_admin', 'platform_admin')`) is the **single source of truth** for the policy and is mirrored verbatim by the 4-role matrix block in `remediation.api.spec.ts` and by the `docs/api-spec.md` + `docs/design.md` text.

### Test cases added/updated (HIGH-2 closeout 4-role matrix)

| # | Test | Role | Expected | Defense-in-depth assertion |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | `auditor → 403 + no state mutation` | `auditor` | 403 | `remaining_quantity` and `status` unchanged after the call |
| 2 | `content_reviewer → 403 + no state mutation` | `content_reviewer` | **403** *(new — load-bearing assertion for the closeout policy)* | `remaining_quantity` and `status` unchanged after the call |
| 3 | `store_admin → 200/201 + claim row + remaining decremented` | `store_admin` | 200/201 | `coupon_id` returned, `remaining_quantity = before - 1` |
| 4 | `platform_admin → 200/201 + claim row + remaining decremented` | `platform_admin` *(new — explicit positive control)* | 200/201 | `coupon_id` returned, `remaining_quantity = before - 1` |

Each test uses an independent coupon (4 codes provisioned in `beforeAll`) so the assertions are deterministic and isolated — no order dependency, no shared state mutation.

### Constraints observed
- ✅ No other security check weakened — store-scope `enforceCouponScope`, hiding policy (404 for foreign-store), and audit logging on claim/redeem/distribute/expire all preserved exactly as-is
- ✅ No tests skipped, no `--force-pass`, no `.skip()`, no temporary TODOs
- ✅ Diff is minimal: 2 source/test files + 2 doc files. The controller change is a single token removed from the `@Roles` decorator; the test change adds 2 new role-coverage tests and tightens the 2 existing ones into a deterministic 4-role matrix
- ✅ No Docker changes — the existing wrapper build/run flow handled the rebuild
